core_mechanism: threshold-gated attractive coupling in a many-body system, where each element adjusts toward others only when within a fixed distance in state space, driving spontaneous relaxation into a discrete set of consensus clusters whose number changes through bifurcations as the interaction-range parameter varies (equivalently described by a master/transport equation for the population density in state space)
brief_reason: The abstract describes agents that gradually adjust their continuous opinions toward others only if they are close enough (bounded confidence), yielding cluster configurations whose bifurcation diagram depends on the confidence bound and which can be reformulated as a master equation on the density in opinion space.
